Package org.eventb.core.ast.expanders

Interface ISmartFactory

All Known Implementing Classes:
PartitionExpander, SmartFactory
public interface ISmartFactory
This interface provides some methods for easily creating optimized predicates and expressions. Instances of this interface can be created using Expanders.getSmartFactory(FormulaFactory).

All predicates and expressions manipulated here must be well-typed.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Predicate
    disjoint(Expression left, Expression right)
    Returns a predicate telling that the two given expressions are disjoint sets.
    Expression
    emptySet(Type type)
    Returns the empty set of the given type.
    Predicate
    equals(Expression left, Expression right)
    Returns a predicate telling that the two given expressions are equal.
    FormulaFactory
    getFormulaFactory()
    Returns the formula factory on which this smart factory is based.
    Expression
    getSingletonMember(Expression expr)
    Returns the only member of the given expression when it is a singleton set.
    Predicate
    in(Expression member, Expression set)
    Returns a predicate telling that the first expression is a member of the second expression.
    Expression
    inter(Type type, Expression... exprs)
    Returns an expression representing the intersection of the arguments.
    Predicate
    land(List<Predicate> predicates)
    Returns the conjunction of the given predicates.
    Predicate
    lor(List<Predicate> predicates)
    Returns the disjunction of the given predicates.
    Predicate
    not(Predicate predicate)
    Returns the negation of the given predicate.
    Expression
    union(Type type, Expression... exprs)
    Returns an expression representing the union of the arguments.

  • Method Details

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Returns the formula factory on which this smart factory is based. The returned formula factory is the one which is used internally to create formulas.
      Returns:
      the underlying formula factory

    • disjoint

      Predicate disjoint(Expression left, Expression right)
      Returns a predicate telling that the two given expressions are disjoint sets. The predicate is optimized for the cases where one or both expressions are a singleton set.
      Parameters:
      left - a set expression of type ℙ(α)
      right - a set expression of type ℙ(α)
      Returns:
      a predicate telling that the two given expressions are disjoint sets

    • getSingletonMember

      Expression getSingletonMember(Expression expr)
      Returns the only member of the given expression when it is a singleton set.
      Parameters:
      expr - a set expression of type ℙ(α)
      Returns:
      the only member of the given expression or null if the given expression is not a singleton set

    • emptySet

      Expression emptySet(Type type)
      Returns the empty set of the given type.
      Parameters:
      type - the type of the result (must be of form ℙ(α))
      Returns:
      the empty set of the given type

    • equals

      Predicate equals(Expression left, Expression right)
      Returns a predicate telling that the two given expressions are equal.
      Parameters:
      left - an expression of type α
      right - an expression of type α
      Returns:
      a predicate telling that the two given expressions are equal

    • inter

      Expression inter(Type type, Expression... exprs)
      Returns an expression representing the intersection of the arguments. The returned expression is optimized for the cases where there are zero or one expression given.
      Parameters:
      type - a type of form ℙ(α)
      exprs - some set expressions of type ℙ(α)
      Returns:
      the intersection of the given expressions

    • in

      Predicate in(Expression member, Expression set)
      Returns a predicate telling that the first expression is a member of the second expression. The returned predicate is optimized for the cases where the second expression is an empty set, a singleton set or a type.
      Parameters:
      member - an expression of type α
      set - a set expression of type ℙ(α)
      Returns:
      a predicate telling that member is a member of set

    • land

      Predicate land(List<Predicate> predicates)
      Returns the conjunction of the given predicates. The result is optimized for the cases where the given list contains zero or one element.
      Parameters:
      predicates - a list of predicates
      Returns:
      the conjunction of the given predicates

    • lor

      Predicate lor(List<Predicate> predicates)
      Returns the disjunction of the given predicates. The result is optimized for the cases where the given list contains zero or one element.
      Parameters:
      predicates - a list of predicates
      Returns:
      the disjunction of the given predicates

    • not

      Predicate not(Predicate predicate)
      Returns the negation of the given predicate. The result is optimized for the case where the given predicate is already negated.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate
      Returns:
      the negation of the given predicate

    • union

      Expression union(Type type, Expression... exprs)
      Returns an expression representing the union of the arguments. The returned expression is optimized for the cases where there are zero or one expression given.
      Parameters:
      type - a type of form ℙ(α)
      exprs - some set expressions (type of form ℙ(α))
      Returns:
      the union of the given expressions
